title: Mule
tileHint: A hill farm where loads are hauled between fields and stone

1.hint: Ask whether the mule would trust your road.
1.successBody: $companionName studies you with long patience, then falls into step as though it had chosen this road before you spoke.
1.failureBody: The mule does not refuse you harshly. It simply remains where the work is still unfinished.

2.hint: Labor with the farm until the burden lightens.
2.successBody: The day wears both of you down in the same honest way. When the load is finally eased, $companionName seems unwilling to part from you.
2.failureBody: The farm comes to respect you, but the mule is still needed where stone and burden meet.

3.hint: Take the steep path and prove you can keep pace.
3.successBody: You take the hardest rise without yielding. When you reach the crest, $companionName is there beside you, breath steaming in the same cold air.
3.failureBody: You test yourself against the steep road and the weight of the day, but the mule remains loyal to the farm that shaped it.

startText:
The farm climbs the land instead of spreading across it. Stone walls bite into the slope, and every burden here seems to have been hauled uphill by stubborn will.

A dark mule stands near a loaded sled, quiet, hard-eyed, and perfectly at home where weaker creatures would already be complaining.

start.text: The mule looks less impressed by you than by the gradient of the hill.

choices.label: Measure the farm before stepping in
Shoulder part of the day's burden

measure1.text: You watch the rhythm of the slope, the pauses before each pull, and the small choices that keep work from becoming waste.

choices.label: Ask about the mule's load and temper
Clear the roughest stretch of the track

shoulder1.text: The farmer says little, only points to the weight that needs moving. The mule keeps one ear turned toward you, judging whether your willingness survives effort.

choices.label: Lift with patience instead of pride
Steady the load when it slips

climb1.text: You choose the steep road first, where stone, mud, and bad footing quickly reveal whether resolve is real or only loud.

choices.label: Push on until the ridge is won
Brace the weight when the path gives way

final.text: By the end of the climb, the mule has reached its judgment.

commitChoices.label: Ask whether the mule would trust your road
Labor with the farm until the burden lightens
Take the steep path and prove you can keep pace
